paper_title: ENTREPRENEURIAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PRIVATE FIRM PERFORMANCE IN THE MEKONG RIVER DELTA

entities.description: Lead author of the study from Can Tho University.

entities.description: The academic institution where the authors are affiliated in Vietnam.
The primary field of research concerning entrepreneurial behavior and firm performance.
Individual traits of managers including tenure, education, and risk aversion.
The dependent variable measuring the success or outcome of a private firm.
A theory suggesting that firm performance reflects the characteristics of its senior leaders.
The tendency of managers to choose less risky solutions.
Ordinary Least Squares regression method used to estimate impacts.
Data collection procedure involving 807 private firms in the Mekong River Delta.
Measurement tool used during the interviews for gathering firm information.
Numerical data collected from 395 private firms in the year 2004.
Numerical data collected from 119 private firms in the year 2009.
Annual economic growth rate of Vietnam mentioned as context.
The participation rate achieved for the 2004 survey data.
The participation rate achieved for the 2009 survey data.
The multiple correlation coefficients indicating model consistency.
Statistical significance values for the regression models.
